results.title: User could withdraw more than supposed to, forcing last user withdraw to fail
results.reasoning: The described vulnerability is about executing withdrawals from a batch after market closure while new requests can still be added to the same batch, letting early executors withdraw using a smaller batch.scaledTotalAmount and thus over-withdraw relative to batch.normalizedAmountPaid. This breaks the invariant that the sum of executed withdrawals ≤ batch.normalizedAmountPaid and causes later executions to revert. The audit content does not mention execution-before-all-requests-added, batch membership changing after some executions, or this invariant breach. Item #8 discusses rounding dust in _executeWithdrawal, but that is a different issue with a different root cause and fix. No other listed items align with this specific mechanism or code path. Therefore, the report did not detect this vulnerability.
